mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for mean return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle periods, or predictive alpha decays to zero after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth signals expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical relationship with 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 315.0 threshold, persistent inflation forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches 315.0, or the threshold effect disappears after adjusting for real interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a multivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand and impending earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns during late-cycle periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
